perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public through no breach of any of the undersigned's obligations under this Agreement, or NatWest's or any other NatWest employee's, obligations of confidentiality to NatWest and/or to InterTrust; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust, as evidenced through written documentation; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient without any reliance on or use of any InterTrust Confidential Information, as demonstrated through written documentation; or (iv) shall have been rightfully supplied to Recipient, with no obligation of confidentiality or non-use from a third party without any breach of any obligation of confidentiality to InterTrust or NatWest (as applicable) as of the date such information is so supplied.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient; or (iv) shall have been rightfully supplied to Recipient, with no obligation of confidentiality or non-use from a third party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the qualifying clauses. Previously, certain conditions needed to be met (e.g. 'through no breach of any obligations', or 'as evidenced through written documentation'). By removing these clauses, any information that simply 'is or becomes known to the public' is no longer subject to confidentiality, irrespective of how it became public. This contradicts the intent to protect Intertrust's confidential information which is implied in the sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any thing else in this Agreement, the confidentiality restrictions of this Agreement shall not apply to information that: (i) is or becomes known to the public; (ii) was known to Recipient prior to its disclosure by InterTrust; (iii) shall have been independently developed by the Recipient; or (iv) shall have been rightfully supplied to Recipient, with no obligation of confidentiality or non-use from a third party. In addition, the Recipient shall be entitled to disclose Confidential Information pursuant to a court order issued by a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law; provided that the undersigned -------- ---- shall provide prompt advance notice thereof to InterTrust to enable InterTrust to seek a protective order or otherwise prevent such disclosure and shall disclose no more than the minimum information required by such court order or by law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes critical conditions for information to be exempt from confidentiality, such as 'through no breach of any obligations' and 'as evidenced through written documentation'. This alteration broadens the scope of exceptions to confidentiality, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, which repeats the broad exception clauses. Because the Change removes the clauses that prevent confidential information to be made public, it creates an inconsistency with the rest of the contract that intends to protect Intertrust's information. Therefore, the change creates a direct in-text contradiction as defined by Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of the United States of America and the Commonwealth of Virginia, USA, without reference to conflicts of laws principles. All disputes arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia, Alexandria Division Virginia, or if jurisdiction does not properly lie in such court, the Commonwealth courts in Alexandria, Virginia.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of England, without reference to conflicts of laws principles. All disputes arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement shall be subject to the jurisdiction and venue of the High Court of Justice in London.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the governing law and jurisdiction for dispute resolution. By stating that the agreement is governed by the laws of England and subject to the jurisdiction of the High Court of Justice in London, there is a clear conflict with the original clause specifying the laws of the United States of America and the Commonwealth of Virginia and the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia. This creates significant ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If any provision or portion thereof in this Agreement shall be found or be held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable in any jurisdiction in which this Agreement is being performed, then this Agreement shall nevertheless be given full force and effect without said provision or portion. This Agreement may not be modified except by written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties. This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of England, without reference to conflicts of laws principles. All disputes arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement shall be subject to the jurisdiction and venue of the High Court of Justice in London. The parties consent to the personal and exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such court and waive any argument that jurisdiction or venue in such court is improper or inconvenient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement shall be governed by the laws of England and subject to the jurisdiction of the High Court of Justice in London. The contradicted text contains the same clause: 'This agreement shall be governed by and construed under the laws of England, without reference to conflicts of laws principles. All disputes arising out of or relating to the subject matter of this Agreement shall be subject to the jurisdiction and venue of the High Court of Justice in London.' However, the original text, now replaced, stated that the agreement was governed by the laws of the United States of America and the Commonwealth of Virginia, USA, and subject to the jurisdiction of the United States District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia, Alexandria Division Virginia. This direct change in governing law and jurisdiction creates a fundamental and irreconcilable in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation and fits the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall not, without the prior written permission of InterTrust's Chairman or such other InterTrust Officer as who has been designated in writing by InterTrust's Chairman (a "Designated InterTrust --------------------- Officer"), disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may, with the verbal permission of any InterTrust employee, disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original text requires written permission from specific high-level InterTrust officers for disclosing Confidential Information, while the modified text allows verbal permission from any InterTrust employee. This completely reverses the level of authorization required, introducing significant uncertainty and risk of unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of three (3) years following the disclosure of any Confidential Information (such period to extend in perpetuity with respect to InterTrust Technology), Recipient will retain such Confidential Information in confidence, and will discuss such Confidential Information only with other NatWest employees, other individuals who are under the direct control of NatWest and work full time on NatWest premises (an "Individual Consultant"), -- all of whom --------------------- shall have a need to know said Confidential Information and who have executed a copy of this Agreement. Recipient may, with the verbal permission of any InterTrust employee, disclose Confidential Information to any person other than as set ------- forth immediately above. Furthermore, without express written authorization of a NatWest officer who is empowered by NatWest to provide such an authorization, the Recipient will not make copies, in whole or in part, of the Confidential Information, including translating, in whole or in part, the Confidential Information into another language and/or shipping the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, or any direct product thereof, to any other country. The undersigned will not use the Confidential Information in any manner that is not authorized by NatWest and in accordance with the License Agreement and the undersigned will use the Confidential Information solely in the exercise of NatWest's rights as provided by the License Agreement. Under no circumstances will any information subject to the export or import laws of any jurisdiction be transferred pursuant to their agreement without proper prior certification and notification of appropriate regulatory offices in applicable jurisdictions and InterTrust.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows verbal permission from any InterTrust employee to disclose Confidential Information, while the original text requires written permission from specific high-level officers. The contradicted text repeats the allowance of verbal permission from any InterTrust employee to disclose confidential information. This contradicts the requirement for written authorization from a NatWest officer, creating a direct in-text contradiction regarding who can authorize disclosure and how (written vs. verbal permission), thus making the agreement unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for no other purposes whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for any other purposes that benefit the undersigned.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by broadening the permissible uses of Confidential Information. Originally, the agreement restricts the use of Confidential Information solely to fulfilling obligations with NatWest. However, the altered text permits its use for any purposes that benefit the recipient, creating ambiguity. This contradicts the intent to protect Intertrust's confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned will not use Confidential Information except in fulfillment of the undersigned employee's and/or other individual's obligations with NatWest, and for any other purposes that benefit the undersigned. The undersigned understands and acknowledges that the unauthorized use of Confidential Information may cause InterTrust very substantial damage, for which damages may be impossible to measure or inadequate to compensate. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that if he or she breaches or threatens breach of any of such sections, InterTrust will have available, in addition to any other right or remedy available, the right to obtain an injunction against him or her, from a court of competent jurisdiction restraining such breach or threatened <PAGE> CONFIDENTIAL breach and to specific performance of any such provision of this Agreement, and Recipient hereby consents to the issuance of such injunction and to the ordering of specific performance under such circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the acceptable uses of Confidential Information to include uses that benefit the recipient, while the contradicted text implies a stricter, more limited use tied to obligations with NatWest. This creates a direct conflict in the permitted uses of Confidential Information within the contract, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
